name: Old Fashioned Apple Cider Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Apples, peeled and thinly sliced (use cooking apples like Mac or Granny Smith)
recipeInstructions:
1. Use cooking apples like McIntosh or Granny Smith. Core and peel, then thinly slice them.
2. Divide the pastry almost in half and roll out the larger half on a lightly floured surface to form a 13-inch circle. Line a 9-inch pie tin with the pastry. Trim the edge 1/2-inch beyond the rim of the pie tin.
3. In a 3-quart saucepan, combine apples, 1 cup apple cider or juice, and sugar. Cook over high heat until the mixture comes to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 8 minutes or until apples are tender. Drain apples, reserving syrup.
4. Add enough additional apple cider to the syrup to make 1 1/3 cups. Return syrup and apples to saucepan. Combine cornstarch and water in a small bowl and stir until well blended. Stir cornstarch mixture and cinnamon into apple mixture.
5. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil. Remove from heat and stir in butter. Pour filling into pastry-lined pie tin.
6. Roll out remaining pastry to an 11-inch circle. Fold pastry gently into quarters and cut slits in folds. Gently unfold on top of filling and trim edge 1 inch beyond rim of pie tin. Fold top crust under lower crust and form a ridge by fluting the edge of the pie.
7. Bake at 400°F for 40-45 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on wire rack until slightly warm before cutting and serving.
